title: lobelia chamaepitys var. ceratophylla
intro: Lobelia chamaepitys var. ceratophylla, also known as small lobelia or spring ghern, is a variety of the Lobelia chamaepitys plant, a low-growing, annual or biennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues and alleviate pain. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease coughs and colds, and even act as a natural diuretic. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to support respiratory health and provide relief from various health issues.
